_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of metadata types (business, technical, operational, process) and the systems and methods used to collect, store, and maintain each type.
comments: DCWF ksa equal WIDAI DQ domain. DQ-K-010 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DG-K-004 (DG, cosine: 0.708), DG-K-011 (DG, cosine: 0.698).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.897 (P=0.953, R=0.848). P exceeds R by 0.105 — the DCWF element's 3 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 21 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). "Knowledge of metadata." and "Knowledge of metadata types (business, technical, operational, process) and the" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.810 > STS 0.655 (gap +0.155). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [knowledge, metadata] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 15.8%. Shared tokens: [knowledge, metadata]. DCWF-only: []. WIDAI-only: [business, collect, maintain, methods, operational, process].
